_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that some materials conduct electricity and some do not, and that electrical circuits have parts that each play a role in making the circuit work.

_source.w: We are learning that some materials conduct electricity and some do not, and that electrical circuits have parts that each play a role in making the circuit work.
y: Understanding circuits helps us know how the electrical devices we use every day actually work.
Knowing about conductors and insulators helps us stay safe around electricity.
This knowledge is essential for careers in engineering, electronics, renewable energy and many trades.
eg: Building a simple circuit with a battery, wires, a switch and a light bulb.
Testing different materials — metal, plastic, wood, rubber — to see which conduct electricity.
Adding a second light bulb to a circuit and noticing that both bulbs are dimmer.
Drawing a circuit diagram using standard symbols for battery, switch and lamp.
Investigating the difference between series and parallel circuits.
Discussing why electricians wear rubber-soled boots and use insulated tools.
